title: Cross-Market Arbitrage (Polymarket vs Kalshi)
description: Exploit price differences for the same event across Polymarket and Kalshi. Buy on one, sell on the other when the spread exceeds transaction costs.
implementationHint: Requires API keys for both platforms and a fast execution engine.

title: Sentiment-Driven News Bot
description: Monitor news feeds for keywords related to active markets. Execute trades based on sentiment scores (positive/negative/neutral).
implementationHint: Use NLP libraries like NLTK or spaCy for sentiment analysis.

title: Whale Tracking Momentum
description: Monitor large wallet addresses on-chain. When a 'whale' takes a significant position, follow their lead if the market hasn't fully reacted.
implementationHint: Use Etherscan API or similar to track specific wallet movements.

title: Political Poll Correlation
description: Trade election markets based on real-time polling data updates. Buy when polls show a candidate gaining momentum before the market adjusts.
implementationHint: Scrape polling aggregators like RealClearPolitics or 538.

title: Liquidity Provision (Market Making)
description: Place limit orders on both sides of the spread to capture the bid-ask spread. Profit from high-volume, low-volatility markets.
implementationHint: Requires a robust risk management system to avoid 'toxic flow'.

title: Technical Analysis Breakout
description: Apply standard TA indicators (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands) to prediction market price charts. Trade on breakouts or trend reversals.
implementationHint: Use libraries like TA-Lib for indicator calculations.

title: Event-Based Hedging
description: Hedge positions in prediction markets with related assets (e.g., candidate-linked tokens or crypto assets).
implementationHint: Calculate hedge ratios based on historical correlation.

title: Social Media Hype Tracker
description: Monitor Reddit and Twitter for mentions of specific markets. Trade on volume spikes before mainstream news picks up the story.
implementationHint: Use Reddit API and Twitter API v2.

title: Order Book Imbalance
description: Analyze the depth of the order book. Trade in the direction of significant buy/sell pressure (imbalance).
implementationHint: Requires real-time WebSocket connection to the order book.

title: Historical Trend Reversion
description: Identify markets that have deviated significantly from their historical mean and trade on the expectation of reversion.
implementationHint: Calculate moving averages and standard deviations.
